design_philosophy.core_loop: Match falling monsters to identical blocks in vertical stacks by swapping adjacent columns; eliminate matches to prevent stacks from growing too tall and crossing the top boundary
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Stack height management under time pressure; player must constantly reorganize columns as new pieces fall unpredictably
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Satisfying cascade elimination and puzzle mastery; special mechanic of hatching Yoshi creatures by encasing monsters between eggshell halves provides narrative flavor to abstract matching
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Four-column falling-block system with swap-based movement (rather than rotation) creates unique spatial puzzle feel distinct from Tetris; Yoshi eggshell mechanic introduces conditional cascades and bonus incentives; designed as revenue generator for Game Freak before Pokémon ambitions

difficulty.continues_system: A-Type mode continues indefinitely until game over; B-Type mode presents discrete level challenges with increasing initial block counts
difficulty.checkpointing: B-Type mode separated into distinct levels; A-Type is continuous until failure
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Two play modes offer different difficulty curves: A-Type for survival-based scoring challenge, B-Type for level-based progression with escalating complexity

progression.unlock_mechanism: B-Type mode progression unlocks successive levels as player clears all blocks from current field; initial block counts increase per level
replayability_hooks: Competitive two-player versus mode where players control separate fields
A-Type endless scoring mode encourages high-score chasing
B-Type level completion provides structured progression challenge
Bonus points for hatching larger Yoshi creatures based on encased monster counts

world_building_techniques: Mario appears as playable character below the field, grounding puzzle mechanics in familiar franchise
Monster enemies (Goombas, etc.) from Mario series contextualize block aesthetics
Yoshi hatching mechanic provides light narrative reward for skilled play
Regional title variations (Japan: 'Yoshi no Tamago' / EU: 'Mario & Yoshi') emphasize character connection

faction_indicators: Two-player competitive mode establishes Mario vs. Luigi rivalry through simultaneous field play
danger_signals: Stack height approaching top boundary creates escalating pressure visualization
Game over condition when any stack crosses black line at field top
environmental_storytelling: Four-column vertical playfield establishes arcade-puzzle aesthetic
Falling monster blocks provide consistent visual feedback of challenge progression
Bonus Yoshi hatching with size variation (small/medium/large) offers visual reward scaling

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Yoshi (NES) article
